question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an arbitrarily large array of timestamps to getContractResultsByTimestamps() causing range(1, timestamps.length + 1) to generate a massive in-memory array and the resulting SQL IN clause to contain thousands of positional parameters, exhausting database query parser memory and crashing the mirror node service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an unbounded involvedContractIds array to getContractResultsByTimestamps() where involvedContractIds.join(',') is directly interpolated into the SQL WHERE clause without parameterization, causing the database to parse an enormous SQL string and triggering a CPU/memory DoS in the PostgreSQL query planner?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a very large timestamps array to getContractLogsByTimestamps() so that range(1, timestamps.length + 1).map((i) => '$' + i) allocates a huge JavaScript array in the Node.js heap, causing out-of-memory crash of the REST service process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an unbounded involvedContractIds array to getContractLogsByTimestamps() where involvedContractIds.join(',') is directly interpolated into the SQL string, producing a query string of arbitrary length that exhausts the database connection buffer and causes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an arbitrarily large timestamps array to getContractStateChangesByTimestamps() causing range(1, timestamps.length + 1) to allocate a large array and the resulting SQL IN clause to contain thousands of bind parameters, exhausting PostgreSQL shared memory and causing a service crash?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an unbounded involvedContractIds array to getContractStateChangesByTimestamps() where involvedContractIds.join(',') is directly interpolated into the SQL condition string, causing the database to receive a query of unbounded length and triggering a CPU-exhausting parse phase DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getContractLogs() to return a very large result set, causing the subsequent rows.forEach() deduplication loop and RecordFileService.getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray(timestamps) call to process an unbounded timestamps array, exhausting Node.js heap memory and crashing the service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query that causes getContractLogs() to collect a large timestamps array from rows and pass it to RecordFileService.getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray(), which then issues a secondary unbounded database query, causing cascading resource exhaustion and a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply lower, inner, and upper filter arrays that each produce a full subquery, causing getContractLogsQuery() to build a three-way UNION query where each branch independently executes contractLogsExtendedQuery with a full entity CTE scan, tripling the database CPU and I/O load and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the UNION branch in getContractLogsQuery() where subQueries.map((q) => '(' + q + ')').join('\\nunion\\n') concatenates multiple full SQL queries into a single string, and the resulting UNION query forces PostgreSQL to sort and deduplicate large intermediate result sets, causing sustained CPU exhaustion and a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user pass timestamp=true to getContractStateByIdAndFilters() triggering the contractStateTimestampQuery path which uses DISTINCT ON (slot) requiring a full sort of the contract_state_change table, causing unbounded CPU and memory usage in PostgreSQL and a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user call getContractStateByIdAndFilters() with an empty whereConditions array and a maximum limit value, causing the contractStateQuery to perform a full table scan of contract_state joined with the entity CTE, returning a massive result set that exhausts Node.js heap memory during rows.map((row) => new ContractState(row))?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an unbounded filters array to getContractActions() where each filter with key === 'index' causes params.push(filter.value) and appends to whereClause in a loop, producing a query string of arbitrary length and an unbounded params array that exhausts Node.js heap memory and crashes the service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a filters array with thousands of index filter entries to getContractActions(), causing the loop to append thousands of AND conditions to whereClause and push thousands of values to params, resulting in a query string that exceeds PostgreSQL's maximum query length and causes a fatal database error that crashes the connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEthereumTransactionsByPayerAndTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user pass arbitrarily large payers and timestamps arrays to getEthereumTransactionsByPayerAndTimestampArray() where both are passed as $1 and $2 array parameters to PostgreSQL, causing the database to evaluate payer_account_id = any($1) and consensus_timestamp = any($2) against enormous arrays, exhausting database CPU and memory in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEthereumTransactionsByPayerAndTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a large timestamps array to getEthereumTransactionsByPayerAndTimestampArray() causing the timestamps.forEach() loop that computes maxTimestamp and minTimestamp to iterate over thousands of entries in the Node.js event loop, blocking the event loop and causing a non-network DoS for all concurrent requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractIdByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a very long hex string as create2_evm_address to getContractIdByEvmAddress() causing Buffer.from(create2EvmAddress, 'hex') to allocate a large buffer in Node.js heap memory, and if called repeatedly at scale, exhausting available heap memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: computeContractIdFromString()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a malformed or extremely long contractIdValue string to computeContractIdFromString() causing EntityId.computeContractIdPartsFromContractIdValue() to perform expensive string parsing operations that block the Node.js event loop, degrading service availability for all concurrent users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByIdAndFiltersQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an unbounded whereConditions array to getContractResultsByIdAndFiltersQuery() causing whereConditions.join(' and ') to produce an arbitrarily long SQL WHERE clause string, exhausting Node.js string memory and causing the database to receive an oversized query that crashes the connection?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getContractResultsByIdAndFilters() with a maximum limit and no filters, causing the query to return a large result set that is then mapped via rows.map((cr) => ({...new ContractResult(cr), hash: cr.hash})), where object spread of each ContractResult exhausts Node.js heap memory and causes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a timestamps array with thousands of entries to getContractResultsByTimestamps() where params = timestamps directly assigns the array as query parameters, causing PostgreSQL to bind thousands of parameters and triggering a query plan explosion that exhausts database CPU in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a large involvedContractIds array to getContractResultsByTimestamps() where the condition ContractResult.CONTRACT_ID in (${involvedContractIds.join(',')}) is built by direct string interpolation without any size limit check, allowing the attacker to craft a query with thousands of literal IDs that causes PostgreSQL query parsing to consume excessive CPU?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a large involvedContractIds array to getContractStateChangesByTimestamps() where the contractIdsQuery string ContractStateChange.CONTRACT_ID in (${involvedContractIds.join(',')}) is built by direct interpolation, and this string is then embedded inside a nested OR condition, causing the database query planner to spend excessive CPU evaluating the complex predicate?
